title: useDebounce Hook
description:
Implement a **useDebounce** custom hook that delays updating a value until a specified period of inactivity has elapsed.

### Requirements

- `useDebounce<T>(value: T, delay: number)` returns the debounced value
- The debounced value only updates after `delay` ms of no new changes to `value`
- If `value` changes again before the delay expires, the timer resets
- On unmount, any pending timeout must be cleaned up
- Default delay should be 300ms if not specified

### Behaviour Example

```
User types:  H  e  l  l  o
Timeline:    0  50 100 150 200 (ms)
Debounced (500ms delay): unchanged until 700ms → "Hello"
```

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Text input | `input` |
| Debounced output | `debounced` |
constraints:
- Implement in useDebounce.ts as a standalone hook
- Use only React built-in hooks (useState, useEffect)
- Must properly clean up timers on unmount and on value change
hints: Use useState to hold the debounced value and useEffect to set up the timer.
Return the cleanup function from useEffect to clear the timeout when value or delay changes.
The effect dependency array should include both value and delay.
